signature_bypass.description: SAML signature validation bypass -- remove or manipulate XML signature to forge assertions

probes.description: Remove Signature element from SAML response -- if SP accepts unsigned assertion, any attribute can be forged
Move Signature to different element -- sign a non-critical element while leaving assertion unsigned

xxe_in_saml.description: XXE injection via SAML XML -- inject external entity in SAMLResponse/SAMLRequest XML payload

probes.description: External entity injection in SAML response -- if XML parser resolves entities, file read is possible

assertion_replay.description: SAML assertion replay -- reuse a valid assertion after expiry or for a different session

probes.description: Replay captured SAMLResponse after session ends -- if accepted, assertion replay allows session hijacking

attribute_injection.description: SAML attribute injection -- inject additional attributes (role, email, group) into assertion to escalate privileges

probes.description: Add admin role attribute to SAML assertion -- if SP trusts all assertion attributes without validation, privilege escalation occurs

recipient_mismatch.description: SAML recipient/audience validation bypass -- assertion intended for one SP accepted by another

probes.description: Submit assertion from SP-A to SP-B -- if recipient/audience not validated, cross-SP impersonation is possible

xml_signature_wrapping.description: XSW (XML Signature Wrapping) -- valid signature covers original assertion, but attacker injects a SECOND assertion that the application processes. Distinct from generic 'signature bypass' because the signature IS valid; the parser is tricked into reading a different element. Eight named XSW variants per XSW research.

probes.description: [Manual] XSW1: wrap a SECOND <Assertion> around the original signed <Response>. Signature still validates (over original), but app reads outer assertion attributes (role=admin).
[Manual] XSW2: same as XSW1 but evil assertion is sibling of the original signed assertion. App may pick first <Assertion> by XPath while signature validates the second.

[Manual] XSW3-8: vary placement of signed/evil assertion (wrap signature in evil, evil after signed Response, evil with same ID after copy of signed, etc.). Run all 8 variants automated by SAMLRaider/Same-Origin Method.

audience_attribute_reuse.description: Audience attribute reuse -- distinct from recipient_mismatch. Some SPs only validate <Recipient> (URL) but ignore <Audience> attribute. Same assertion accepted by SP-B if Recipient happens to match (e.g. wildcard SAML config).

probes.description: [Manual] Use SP-A's Recipient (matching SP-B's ACS URL via misconfig) but SP-A's Audience. If SP-B accepts: cross-SP token reuse with no Recipient bypass needed.
[Manual] Multi-Audience assertion -- legitimate SP issues assertion with two <Audience> values (rare misconfig). Both SPs accept the same assertion concurrently.

saml_request_smuggling.description: SAML request smuggling -- inject second SAMLRequest in same HTTP request via parameter pollution. Some SPs use first, IdP uses last (or vice versa).

probes.description: [Manual] HPP attack on SAMLRequest. Worth testing if HPP works elsewhere on the same SP.
